editor_settings.title: Gigabyte AORUS Master RTX 4090 Unboxing: Why This Card Still Feels High-End
editor_settings.content:
Gigabyte AORUS Master RTX 4090 Unboxing: Why This Card Still Feels High-End

The AORUS Master RTX 4090 still has that “beast GPU” energy.

You see the size, the cooler, the branding, and the 24GB memory story, and it makes sense why people still search for it.

But the smart buyer question is simple: is it still worth chasing now?

The TikTok Sells The Dream Well

This unboxing frames the AORUS RTX 4090 exactly how most buyers imagine it: 4K gaming, content creation, ray tracing, DLSS 3, big cooling, and future-proofing.

@grigobyte 📦 Unboxing the beastly and stunning @NVIDIA GeForce GeForce RTX 4090 @AORUS @GIGABYTE AORUS MASTER. This GPU is made for those who demand nothing but the best - whether you’re gaming at 4K, creating content, or pushing your system to its limits. With features like 24 GB of GDDR6X memory, real-time ray tracing, and AI-driven DLSS 3.0, this card offers next-level performance and visual fidelity. Its powerful cooling system ensures it stays cool even during the most intense gaming sessions. If you’re serious about performance and future-proofing your build, this is the upgrade you need! What do you think about the RTX 4090 graphics card? Let us know below. #pc #pcbuild #pcsetup #gamingpc #tech #computer #gpu #graphicscard #aorus #gigabyte #nvidia #geforce ♬ BABYDOLL (Speed) - Ari Abdul

That is the whole RTX 4090 appeal in one clip.

It is not a normal upgrade. It is the kind of card people buy when they want their PC to stop feeling limited.

Why The AORUS Master Still Matters

The RTX 4090 is still powerful because of three things:

  • 24GB of GDDR6X memory
  • high-end 4K gaming performance
  • strong creator and AI headroom

The AORUS Master version adds the big-card treatment: heavy cooling, premium design, and a more serious board-partner build than basic models.

That matters if your PC runs long gaming sessions, video exports, rendering jobs, or AI tasks where heat and noise become annoying.

Who This Card Is For

This is not for casual 1080p gaming.

It makes sense for:

  • 4K gaming with ray tracing
  • 1440p high refresh gaming with lots of headroom
  • video editing and 3D rendering
  • local AI experiments that need VRAM
  • builders who want a premium-looking GPU

If your monitor is 1080p 60Hz, skip it. You will be paying for power you barely touch.

The Price Has To Be Right

The AORUS Master RTX 4090 can still be a great buy, but only at the right price.

In 2026, RTX 5090 changed the top-end comparison. Newer cards bring newer architecture, more VRAM on the 5090, and stronger AI-focused positioning.

So the AORUS 4090 has to win on value. If it is only a small discount versus a 5090, I would not force it.

[product-card:asus-tuf-gaming-oc-geforce-rtx-5090-32gb-graphics-card-32gb-gddr7-pcie-50-21760-cuda-cores-8k-support-triple-fan-cooling]


Final Verdict

The Gigabyte AORUS Master RTX 4090 still feels high-end because it is high-end.

It is excellent for 4K gaming, heavy creator work, and anyone who wants 24GB VRAM without jumping to the newest flagship.

But the deal matters more than the name. Buy it discounted. Avoid it overpriced.

Sources Checked

  • NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4090 official page
  • NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090 official page
  • ASUS ROG Strix RTX 4090 specs
  • Gigabyte AORUS RTX 4090 Master product page
